rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memoranda Of Csots Fro John J. Hartford And Albert Lee,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH KARNAZES' Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memoranda Of Costs of John J. Hartford And Albert Lee. Denied. The Memorandum of Costs ("Costs Memo") is timely. (CRC 3.1700(a)(1).) On January 25, 2012, the Court issued an order granting Defendant Hartford's motion to quash service of summons based on a lack of personal delivery. (Order issued, Jan. 25, 2012.) This is not a judgment or dismissal pursuant to Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1700(a)(1).) For Costs Memo Item no. 1, Defendant Hartford is entitled to filing costs. (CCP §1033.5(a)(1).) For Costs Memo Item no. 11, the Court determines that Defendant Hartford may recover costs for messenger services. (Foothill-De Anza Community College Dist. v. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 30.) For Memorandum of Costs on Appeal ("Appeal Costs Memo") Item no. 1, Defendant Hartford is entitled to filing costs (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(A). For Appeal Costs Memo Item no. 4: Defendant Hartford is entitled to costs to print and reproduce his briefs. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(E).) For Appeal Costs Memo Item no. 7, Defendant Hartford is entitled to service costs. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(D).) For Appeal Costs Memo Item no. 8, Defendant Hartford is entitled to costs to serve his briefs and other papers. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(D). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JORDAN ROSENBERG VS. CORNELL CORRECTIONS OF CALIFORNIA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG's Motion To Tax Costs. Denied. Defendants are entitled to recover copying costs of $ 539.75 for the clerk's transcript. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(B).) Defendants did not waive the copying costs in writing. Defendants' emails contemplate the shipping costs associated with borrowing the transcript only. Printing and copying costs of $ 0.25 per page is reasonable. Therefore, Defendants are also entitled to recover printing costs of $ 310.00 for briefs. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(E).) Defendants are entitled to recover costs of $ 57.62 for overnight delivery. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(D).) rulings.Case Title: JACK OSWALD et al VS. MITCHELL MENAGED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Pursuant To Ccp Section 1985.3.
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MITCHELL MENAGED's Motion To Quash Subpoena Pursuant To Ccp Section 1985.3. Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Quash Subpoena is granted. The motion is not opposed. From the file it would appear that the motion has merit and, if opposed, might well have been granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: NEXTG NETWORKS OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 4, PETITIONER CROWN CASTLE NG WEST INC.'s Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or Writ Of Mandate. Granted. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Respondents abused their discretion in considering the late-filed appeal to the Department of Public Works' issuance of Wireless Box Permit No. 10WR-0021. The record shows that the appeal period had expired, and the City had not intentionally or inadvertently cause the requestor to be late in filing the appeal. (See Administrative Record, 1; see also Petitioner's Request For Judicial Notice, Exh, C, Rules of the Board of Appeals, Section 10(a). Under then-existing law, the lack of notice was not grounds for acceptance of an untimely appeal. Petitioner is ordered to submit Wireless Box Permit No. 10WR-0021 to the San Francisco Planning Department for review which was required under then-existing law, Administrative Code Section 11.9, and under current law, Public Works Code, Article 25. (Administrative Code 351-352.) rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTs WILLIAM HEDDEN, MARCUS MERCHASIN and CONSOLIDATED ADJUSTING, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. Defendant's reply relies on case law holding that litigation ending with a negotiated settlement is not a favorable termination because it does not reflect the merits of the underlying action but instead is the result of a compromise between the parties. Ferreira v. Gray, Cary, Ware & Freidenrich (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 409, 413; see also Dalany v. American Pac. Holding Corp., (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 822, 828-29. This reliance is misplaced. Both of the cases cited by Defendant terminated as a result of negotiated settlements. Here, in contrast, the parties litigated the action to a final judgment on the merits. The $16,323 payment to Defendant does not change the character of the termination of this action because the malicious prosecution alleged by Plaintiff relates to the $1 million default judgment awarded to and pursued for over ten years by Defendant subsequent to Defendant's receipt of the amount that the parties agreed that Defendant was owed. Thus, Plaintiff did not challenge nor seek return of that amount in its action. Moreover, the judgment in the underlying case finds that the $16,323 was a full settlement for the 10% agreed on by the parties as well as the $7000 advanced to Plaintiff. Likewise this judgment should not effect the character of Plaintiff as the "prevailing party" in the underlying case because this amount was, if ever, negotiated and settled prior to the alleged malicious prosecution by Defendant for the $1 million judgment. The judgment in the underlying case specifically holds that the Plaintiff (now Defendant) "takes nothing." Consolidated Adjusting v. Miller-Galande Antiques (2010) Case No. 302473 Judgment at 1; see also Complaint 53, 54, 60, 61 (pleading elements). All requests for judicial notice are granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THOMPSON BELLINGRATH VS. FASTER LIGHTER BETTER, INC, DBA COPPER.IS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Thompson Bellingrath'S Motion To Reopen Discovery On A Limited Basis
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 10, Plaintiff Thompson Bellingrath'S Motion To Reopen Discovery On A Limited Basis Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This is an action to recover compensation due to plaintiff for inter alia services rendered pursuant to an oral agreement that was apparently confirmed by email from defendant. The discovery motion at issue here is one by plaintiff to reopen discovery on a limited basis to obtain copies of 6 "cancelled" (it is not clear why they were cancelled) checks written by defendant to "me [plaintiff] and Scott [whose identity is not specified but apparently is plaintiff's business partner] in the amount of $6000.00 each, dated from January 2012 to March 2012, which discovery request, while arguably tardy and time barred, as contended by defendant, would, plaintiff contends, assist him in establishing his claim for compensation under the oral agreement. Throughout the course of his action against defendant plaintiff has alternatively appeared pro per and by at least 2 other counsel. During this time period defendant has also pursued a motion to compel arbitration of plaintiff's claim, which motion was ultimately denied on appeal. If there has been some possible tardiness in plaintiff's motion to compel limited discovery here, that may be explained at least in part by the somewhat convoluted procedural history here. Plaintiff's discovery request here seems objectively to be both very limited, justified, and clearly directed toward evidence (the checks) that appear to be useful in making his case. Therefore, plaintiff's motion is granted. rulings.Case Title: CARLOS ALBERTO RAMIREZ VERA VS. ELIZABETH L. THIERIOT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF CARLOS VERA'S Motion To Enforce Settlement. Granted. (CCP § 664.6.) The Settlement Agreement was signed by all parties and includes an integration clause. (Settlement Agreement, § 15.) Defendant counsel's letter enclosed with the signed Settlement Agreement is not a part of the Settlement Agreement. Defendant counsel's unilateral attempt to add a provision regarding the responsibility of all liens is improper. If there were concerns with any provision or omission in the Settlement Agreement, Defendants should not have signed the Settlement Agreement. Therefore, within 5 days of this order, Defendants Elizabeth L. Thieriot and Joshua David Horowitz or their insurer shall issue a check in the amount of $ 29,000.00 made payable to "Samuel Kornhauser and Carlos Ramirez Vera." Furthermore, Plaintiff is entitled to reasonable attorney fees and costs for bringing this Motion to Enforce Settlement. (Settlement Agreement, § 12.) Defendants Elizabeth L. Thieriot and Joshua David Horowitz or their insurer shall pay Plaintiff's attorney fees and costs in the amount of $ 1,960.00 to be paid within 30 days of service of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, PART 1 OF TENTATIVE RULING FOR Line 3,PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH'S Motion For Entry Of Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties have negotiated an agreed protective order except for a provision therein requested by defendant that would include the following information in and among that which would be subject to confidential treatment under the proposed protective order: "Public business, proprietary or competitive information that, if disseminated, could result in competitive harm to the disclosing party." Apparently, defendant contends that, even though the information is in the public domain, it should be accorded the protections of the protective order. Plaintiff contends that once information is in the public domain it loses any protections that such information may have been afforded prior to proper public disclosure. Plaintiff has moved for the entry of a protective order in the form of an attached 14 page protective order which is presumably that which the parties have negotiated and agreed without the above quoted provision which defendant has proposed and which is at issue here, although it is not clear whether that presumption on my part is correct. The draft provision, upon analysis, is not as clear nor specific as it might be and the instant disagreement over the propriety of its inclusion should be dissipated by some possibly more nuanced and specific drafting. For example, proprietary, and to a somewhat lesser extent, competitive, information, is ordinarily thought of as being non-public and therefore distinct from "public business" information, even though in the draft provision, they are all lumped together. Accordingly, I make no tentative decision on plaintiff's motion and rule that a hearing is in order to explore further the issues created by the draft protective order provision. Prior to the hearing I would encourage the parties to (using a really outdated expression) "take pen to paper" in order to resolve the current impasse and reach agreement at the hearing on that provision and the entire protective order without further burdening the court in a matter that can and should be resolved with some more careful and inventive draftsmanship. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Defts Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For An Awartd Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defts And Their Counsel In Trhe Amount Of $2716.25
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, PART 1 OF TENTATIVE RULING FOR Line 5, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Defts Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For An Awartd Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defts And Their Counsel In Trhe Amount Of $2716.25 Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: A lengthy deposition of Defendant Boyer as a PMK has already been taken and defendants have produced numerous documents. On or about October 29, 2013 Judge Pro Tem Robert Kane signed an order in which the parties agreed that defendants had produced all documents necessary and that all issues that Plaintiff had with Defendants' document production had been resolved. Defendants thereafter produced further documents. Plaintiff wanted a further deposition of defendant in the fall of 2013 which defendant Boyer was then willing to submit to subject to the condition that it had to be done fairly quickly because he had to take a trip to Brazil. Despite defendants' numerous efforts to schedule such a deposition plaintiff was unable to agree on such a date before the end of 2013. Plaintiff renewed its efforts to take Boyer's deposition in early 2014, but by then he had moved to Brazil. Plaintiff insists that the deposition take place in Berkeley CA. Boyer is willing to come to Berkeley so long as plaintiff pays, but plaintiff refuses to so pay. Defendant suggests that his deposition can take place in Brazil or by Skype. The motion is denied subject to the following conditions. 1. Plaintiff and defendant Boyer shall participate in a further meet and confer on or before April 1, 2014 to reach a mutually acceptable further deposition venue and date, said deposition to be taken by on or June 1, 2014. 2. Defendant Boyer is to disclose to plaintiff when he might be in California or elsewhere in the US prior to June 1, 2014 when his deposition could be taken in the US, each party to assume its cost of such deposition. 3. Boyer make himself available in Sao Paulo, Brazil for his deposition in Sao Paulo by plaintiff on or before June 1, 2014, the costs and expenses of the deposition that each party may incur to be for the account of each such party. 4. On or before June 1, 2014, Boyer make himself available in Sao Paulo, Brazil, for a deposition via Skype to be arranged by plaintiff, each party to assume its own costs therefor. 5. No monetary sanctions are to be payable by either party. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: VANCE S. ELLIOTT VS. DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS BENEFITS MANAGEMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS BENEFITS MANAGEMENT's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: JERRY RODRIGUEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer to the retaliation (Gov't 9149.20 et seq.) cause of action without leave to amend. Gov't 9149.23(a) provides: "An employee may not directly or indirectly use or attempt to use the official authority or influence of the employee for the purpose of intimidating, threatening, coercing, commanding, or attempting to intimidate, threaten, coerce, or command any person for the purpose of interfering with the right of that person to disclose to a legislative committee improper governmental activities." (emphasis added). In this case, plaintiff has no allegations that he reported misconduct to a legislative committee. The Court sustains the demurrer to the wrongful termination in violation of public policy cause of action without leave to amend. That common law cause of action is not cognizable against defendant, a public entity. See Miklosy v. Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 900. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. NELSON BROTHERS PROFESSIONAL REAL ESTATE, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC'S Motion For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's moved for evidentiary sanctions against defendant to exclude any future testimony by Patrick Nelson, a principal of defendant. A January 17, 2014 court order directed defendant to produce for deposition a "witness ... pursuant to plaintiff's September 10, 2013 deposition notice." Plaintiff argues that the order, stipulation and the law required that defendant produce as a deposition witness a person "most qualified to testify". Defendant produced Beau Jaussi, an apparently well prepared, qualified and relatively senior representative of defendant, who apparently was party to, or copied on, much of the correspondence, electronic or otherwise, in which Patrick Nelson was involved, or to which he was privy, relating to the sales transaction at issue in the case. He also had been intimately involved in the sales transaction. Plaintiff's deposition of Mr. Jaussi lasted approximately 7 hours. Toward the end of the deposition, plaintiff's counsel objected to Mr. Jaussi as a deposition witness and said he believed that Patrick Nelson should be produced as the witness in response to the Notice of Deposition. While disagreeing at the deposition that Mr. Nelson should have been the witness that day, counsel for defendant nevertheless offered to produce Mr. Nelson as a witness at a date agreeable to the parties and followed up that offer the next day. Counsel for defendant later asked for a meet and confer about availability when plaintiff's counsel questioned whether defendant would actually produce Mr. Nelson at a deposition. Instead of making arrangements to depose Mr. Nelson or even discussing the matter about available dates, plaintiff brought the instant motion for sanctions. In view of the foregoing circumstances, plaintiff's motion for evidentiary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: VASU D. ARORA VS. ARNOLD LAUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTs LAW OFFICES OF ARNOLD LAUB and 807 MONTGOMERY STREET ASSOCIATES LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) The Court takes judicial notice of the First Amended Complaint filed in SF Superior Court case CGC-10-501595. As to the first cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims involve the same primary right, inadequate payment of wages and insufficient meal and rest breaks, raised in previous litigation against Defendant (SF Superior Court case CGC-10-501595), and therefore are barred by res judicata. Alternatively, Plaintiff's claims are barred by the Settlement Agreement entered into on January 6, 2012, in which Plaintiff agreed to release Defendant from "any claims arising out of Employee's employment relationship with the Company." (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), Ex. 5, Settlement Agreement, par. 11.) As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to clearly allege, if Plaintiff can in good faith, how Defendant breached the Settlement Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DEANNA MANZO et al VS. PALACE HOTEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses;
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS KYO-YA COMPANY LTD. AND STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses; Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: In this slip and fall action defendants have moved for an order compelling dismissal of the action as a terminating sanction, or in the alternative, compelling outstanding discovery requests of defendants as well as for monetary sanctions in the total amount of $3,915.00. There was a previous action involving virtually the same parties in which the plaintiff dismissed the action on January 22, 2012, after defendants' motion to compel discovery and for monetary sanctions was granted by the court. The action was refiled on June 30, 2013 in which plaintiff's husband was added as a plaintiff. Defendants thereupon filed a responsive pleading and discovery requests. Plaintiffs have failed to date to respond to the discovery requests and meet and confer requests. There is nothing in the court file on the motion indicating any response or correspondence from plaintiffs or their counsel to the discovery requests or emails from defendants' counsel on the discovery or on any other aspect of the action that plaintiffs have brought against defendants. There is silence and no apparent activity on the part of the plaintiffs in furthering their prosecution of the action they have brought against defendants. Accordingly, plaintiffs' motion for terminating sanctions against plaintiffs and dismissing the action against defendants is granted. Plaintiffs' motion for monetary sanctions against plaintiffs in the total amount of $3,915.00 is also granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: GREG BURGHART VS. DANIEL O'NEILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Limit Discovery, Et Al.
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 11, 2014, PART 1 OF TENTATIVE RULING FOR Line 9, DEFENDANTS DANIEL O'NEILL AND CINDY O'NEILL'S Motion For Protective Order To Limit Discovery, Et Al. Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's action is for damages for alleged habitability defects in an allegedly unpermitted "in law" apartment unit brought by plaintiff tenant after his tenancy therein was terminated. The motion in question here is one by the defendants for a protective order to limit discovery with specific reference to 1) plaintiff's effort to conduct inspection of real property [the entire building in which the unit was located] other than the dwelling unit that is the subject of the pending action; 2) preclude the deposition of defendant's counsel; and 3) preclude discovery into the contents of defendant's counsel's files to protect her attorney client privileged communications and attorney work product. In addition, defendant's motion seeks monetary sanctions. Defendants have resisted plaintiff's request and efforts to obtain discovery by way of inspection of most if not all of the entire building owned by defendant O'Neil in which plaintiff's unit was located. Defendant has nevertheless said that plaintiff may make a discovery inspection of the unit itself in which plaintiff had lived. Defendant O'Neil has also resisted (on attorney client privilege grounds) plaintiff's efforts to obtain the deposition of defendant's personal counsel who had some involvement in the matter as well as plaintiff's request for the content of that counsel's files that defendants justifiably assert are protected by the attorney client privilege and constitute protected (from such discovery) attorney work product. Defendants' assertion (not clearly rebutted by plaintiff) is that inter alia information or documents relevant to the matter that the counsel may have are also largely in the possession of defendant O'Neil and/or his daughter, also a defendant in the case, and with whom the counsel also worked in connection with this matter. The motion is granted in all respects except that defendants' request for monetary sanctions against plaintiff is denied. The parties shall conduct a meet and confer meeting by no later than April 1, 2014 to arrange for plaintiff's discovery inspection of his former apartment unit which inspection shall take place by on or before May 1, 2014.
